{"query":"Justice Elena Kagan Supreme Court ethics code enforcement remarks Samuel Alito concert tickets German princess 2024 report","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1635,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-justice-samuel-alito-german-princess-900-concert-tickets-rcna170019","title":"Samuel Alito reports German princess gave him $900 concert tickets","content":"WASHINGTON — Justice Samuel Alito reported Friday that he accepted $900 worth of concert tickets from a German princess, but disclosed no trips paid for by other people, according to a new financial disclosure form.
Alito didn’t report any outside income from teaching or book contracts.
Alito, meanwhile, took a private plane trip to a luxury Alaska fishing lodge from two wealthy Republican donors in in 2008, the nonprofit investigative news site ProPublica reported last year.
Jones that was originally worth between $250,000 and $500,000 has now been mostly paid down, but was inadvertently omitted from some of his past reports.
Some Democrats, including President Joe Biden, have pressed for the adoption of a binding code of conduct and provide for investigations of alleged violations.
Concert tickets were also disclosed by another justice, Ketanji Brown Jackson, this year — hers were a gift from the singer Beyoncé, valued at more than $3,700."},{"url":"https://www.politico.com/news/2024/07/25/elena-kagan-supreme-court-ethics-reform-00171317","title":"Elena Kagan calls for better enforcement of Supreme Court’s ethics code - POLITICO","content":"Kagan publicly endorsed calls for a system to enforce the code of ethics the court adopted last year after a raft of bad publicity about ethics issues involving her colleagues led to outrage from Congress and tarnished the court’s prestige.
Kagan made no direct reference Thursday to the ethics claims that have swirled over two of her conservative colleagues over the past couple of years: Alito was accused of failing to report a private airplane flight to Alaska paid for by a wealthy Republican donor, and Justice Clarence Thomas faced stories disclosing that he’d vacationed by private yacht with another major GOP donor."},{"url":"https://truthout.org/articles/justice-elena-kagan-says-she-backs-enforcement-rules-for-scotus-ethics-code/","title":"Justice Elena Kagan Says She Backs Enforcement Rules for SCOTUS Ethics Code | Truthout","content":"During an NPR interview earlier this month, Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, the newest member of the high court, implied that she, too, would back the idea of a panel enforcing the ethics rules, although not as forcefully as Kagan has."},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/25/politics/kagan-supreme-court-ethics-sacramento-conference","title":"Justice Elena Kagan says Supreme Court’s code of conduct needs an enforcement plan. Takeaways from her wide-ranging comments. | CNN Politics","content":"Justice Elena Kagan says Supreme Court’s code of conduct needs an enforcement plan.
Kagan’s criticism of the court on Thursday was not limited to its new code of conduct."},{"url":"https://www.wsls.com/news/national/2024/07/25/justice-kagan-says-there-needs-to-be-a-way-to-enforce-the-us-supreme-courts-new-ethics-code/","title":"Justice Kagan says there needs to be a way to enforce the US Supreme Court's new ethics code","content":"SACRAMENTO, Calif. – Justice Elena Kagan on Thursday became the first member of the U.S.
Supreme Court to call publicly for beefing up its new ethics code by adding a way to enforce it.
In her first public remarks since the nation's highest court wrapped up its term earlier this month, Kagan said she wouldn't have signed onto the new rules if she didn't believe they were good.
In June, a survey for The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research found that 4 in 10 U.S. adults have hardly any confidence in the justices and 70% believe they are more likely to be guided by their own ideology rather than serving as neutral arbiters.
Kagan, who was nominated to the Supreme Court in 2010 by then-President Barack Obama, said Thursday that having a way to enforce the ethics code would also protect justices if they are wrongly accused of misconduct.
And she said justices should not “use individual cases as vehicles to advance some broader agenda.”"}]}
